name: Caching System
description: TTL-based smart caching layer with automatic invalidation, request deduplication, and stale-while-revalidate strategy.

intent.what: Provides a generic caching layer for database queries with configurable TTL, automatic invalidation on mutations, and request deduplication for concurrent calls.
intent.why: Reduces database load, improves response times, and provides a clean abstraction for cache management without manual key tracking.

steps: Create cache instance or use getInstance() for singleton
Use get() to retrieve and set() to store
Use invalidate() or invalidatePattern() to clear entries

internal_flow: get(): Check store, verify not expired, increment hits, return data
set(): Evict old entries if at max, store with expiration
invalidatePattern(): Iterate keys, match pattern, delete matches

rules: Eviction removes 20% of entries (soonest to expire)
anti_patterns: Don't use very short TTLs (< 1 second)
Don't use overly broad invalidation patterns

when_to_use: Use for caching database query results
Use for API responses that don't change frequently
when_not_to_use: Don't use for real-time data streams
Don't use as primary data store
reasoning_strategy: Determine appropriate TTL based on data volatility
Use specific string patterns for invalidation
Monitor cache stats to tune maxEntries and TTL
For query caching, prefer queryEngine (handles automatically)
